member.name: M:StreamMapperTotals.CommonFunctions.GetValueAtTime_old(OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint,PITimeServer.PITime,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Gets the first PI event of a Tag near the specified time or returns nothing. Searches back from the specified time first, and if nothing is found searches forward. Note: Code has got stuck in a loop before - but was not resolved
member.param: The PIAcePoint of interest.
The time to retrieve the value from as PITime - use PIACEBIFunctions.ParseTime(...,...) to convert from date time to PITime

Time match (negative) tolerance in seconds as double. ie. will return an event within (+)-0.5 seconds of the GetTime If pTol is specified the returned event will have a time stamp within GetTime - Tol and GetTime + pTol
Time match positive tolerance in seconds as double. ie. returned event will have a time stamp within GetTime - Tol and GetTime + pTol
member.returns: PIACE event with value and time, or nothing is the data is bad or does not exist
member.remarks: updated 2009-06-24 08:44 Note: Code has got stuck in a loop before - but was not resolved (loop break after 100 iterations has been implemented)

member.name: M:StreamMapperTotals.CommonFunctions.GenerateFiveAMYesterdayDates(System.Object)
member.summary: Generates two dates, the first of which is 05:01am of at least 24 hours prior, the second of which is 05:00am the next day.
member.returns: A Date time Array, index 0 = time at start of period; index 1 = time at end of period
member.remarks: An older function that only works for 1 day periods from 05:00:01 to 05:00:00 - use "GetTotalizedPeriod" for a more options updated 2009-03-31 10:30

member.name: M:StreamMapperTotals.CommonFunctions.SendViaPISDK(System.String,System.String,System.Object,System.DateTime)
member.summary: This method saves the value of the tag at the given time using the PI SDK (as opposed to the standard ACE method). This is useful for saving the value at an exact time, with no drift; or writing multiple values into PI.
member.param: The tag name as a string.
The tag's value.
The time at which this value occured.
member.remarks: The PI ACE calculation context needs to run off the modular database, and there needes to be 1 alias in the root node of the context for this method to work. The PI server for PIAlias.Item(1) is used for all output tags - this will result in errors if tags from multiple servers are present updated 2009-03-31 10:30 updated 2011-11-30 got servername from context path finds tag directly from this

member.name: M:StreamMapperTotals.CommonFunctions.SendViaPISDK(PISDK.PIAlias,System.Object,System.DateTime,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.DateTime,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Writes out a value to PI, with option to test (no writeout), check value against the tags zero and span, write to a second date
member.param: PI ACE Alias to write to
Value to write out
Date Time to write to
true = running a test and no value is writen; false = write a value out
true = check the input Value to see if it is with the zero and span range If less than TagZero then "Under Range" is writen out If greater than TagZero + TagSpan then "Over Range" is writen out
optinal second date to write the input value to
specify a zero to overide the TagZero or when TagZero is known PIACEPoint.Zero
specify a span to overide the TagSpan or when TagSpan is known PIACEPoint.Span

member.name: M:StreamMapperTotals.CommonFunctions.SendViaPISDK(PISDK.PIPoint,System.Object,System.DateTime,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.DateTime,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Writes out a value to PI, with option to test (no writeout), check value against the tags zero and span, write to a second date
member.param: PI Point to write to
Value to write out
Date Time to write to
true = running a test and no value is writen; false = write a value out
true = check the input Value to see if it is with the zero and span range If less than TagZero then "Under Range" is writen out If greater than TagZero + TagSpan then "Over Range" is writen out
optinal second date to write the input value to
specify a zero to overide the TagZero or when TagZero is known PIACEPoint.Zero
specify a span to overide the TagSpan or when TagSpan is known PIACEPoint.Span

member.name: M:StreamMapperTotals.CommonFunctions.SendViaPISDK(OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint,System.Object,System.DateTime,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.DateTime,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Writes out a value to PI, with option to test (no writeout), check value against the tags zero and span, write to a second date
member.param: PI ACE Point to write to
Value to write out
Date Time to write to
true = running a test and no value is writen; false = write a value out
true = check the input Value to see if it is with the zero and span range If less than TagZero then "Under Range" is writen out If greater than TagZero + TagSpan then "Over Range" is writen out
optinal second date to write the input value to
specify a zero to overide the TagZero or when TagZero is known PIACEPoint.Zero
specify a span to overide the TagSpan or when TagSpan is known PIACEPoint.Span

member.name: M:StreamMapperTotals.CommonFunctions.SendViaPISDK(System.String,System.Object,System.DateTime,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.DateTime,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Writes out a value to PI, with option to test (no writeout), check value against the tags zero and span, write to a second date
member.param: full PITag name to write to //server/Tag
Value to write out
Date Time to write to
true = running a test and no value is writen; false = write a value out
true = check the input Value to see if it is with the zero and span range If less than TagZero then "Under Range" is writen out If greater than TagZero + TagSpan then "Over Range" is writen out
optinal second date to write the input value to
specify a zero to overide the TagZero or when TagZero is known PIACEPoint.Zero
specify a span to overide the TagSpan or when TagSpan is known PIACEPoint.Span

member.name: M:StreamMapperTotals.CommonFunctions.writePISDK(PISDK.PIPoint,System.Object,System.DateTime,System.Boolean)
member.summary: This method saves the value of the tag at the given time using the PI SDK It uses a PIPoint passed into the function and is more efficient than SendViaPISDK
member.param: The tag name as a string. = PIAlias.DataSource
The tag's value.
The time at which this value occurred.
False = Value written to PI, True = no value written to PI
member.remarks: This method should be used as the preferred write out as it is more efficient that SendViaPISDK (which has to first search for the tag using a string input) Writes out when the PI SDK tag is know, the SDK tag is available from the PIAlias.DataSource property writePISDK(PIAlias.DataSource, 23, 2010-08-17 06:00:01) updated 2010-08-17

member.name: M:StreamMapperTotals.CommonFunctions.UpdateViaPISDDKWithCheck(PISDK.PIPoint,System.Object,System.DateTime,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.DateTime,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Writes out a value to PI, with option to test (no writeout), check value against the tags zero and span, write to a second date
member.param: PIpoint to write to
Value to write out
Date Time to write to
true = running a test and no value is writen; false = write a value out
true = check the input Value to see if it is with the zero and span range If less than TagZero then "Under Range" is writen out If greater than TagZero + TagSpan then "Over Range" is writen out
optinal second date to write the input value to
specify a zero to overide the TagZero or when TagZero is known PIACEPoint.Zero
specify a span to overide the TagSpan or when TagSpan is known PIACEPoint.Span

member.name: M:StreamMapperTotals.CommonFunctions.RemoveViaPISDK(OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint,System.DateTime,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.DateTime,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Removes a value from PI, either the first value whith multivalue times or all values
member.param: AcePoint to Remove value from
Date to remove value
true = running a test and no value is writen; false = write a value out
true = removes all values at the time True = remove all values False = remove first value
optinal second date to remove value from
optional remove a range of values first date to second date

member.name: M:StreamMapperTotals.CommonFunctions.RemoveViaPISDK(PISDK.PIAlias,System.DateTime,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.DateTime,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Removes a value from PI, either the first value whith multivalue times or all values
member.param: PIAlias to Remove value from
Date to remove value
true = running a test and no value is writen; false = write a value out
true = removes all values at the time True = remove all values False = remove first value
optinal second date to remove value from
optional remove a range of values first date to second date

member.name: M:StreamMapperTotals.CommonFunctions.RemoveViaPISDDKWithCheck(PISDK.PIPoint,System.DateTime,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.DateTime,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Removes a value from PI, either the first value whith multivalue times or all values
member.param: PIpoint to Remove valuye from
Date to remove value
true = running a test and no value is writen; false = write a value out
true = removes all values at the time True = remove all values False = remove first value
optinal second date to remove value from
optional remove a range of values first date to second date

member.name: M:StreamMapperTotals.CommonFunctions.GetTotalizedPeriod(OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint@,System.DateTime,System.DateTime)
member.summary: A method to calculate the event-weighted total of all numeric samples within the given period.
member.param: The tag which will be totalized.
The start time
The end time.
member.remarks: Sep 20 2012 was converting to string before adding. so values where concatenating and not adding. Sep 28 2012 was adding to double.NaN.

member.name: M:StreamMapperTotals.CommonFunctions.GetTotValue(OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint,PITimeServer.PITime)
member.summary: Get a the last finalised-Totaliser-value before the given time using the tag attributes If time is 06:32 and the totaliser ends at 06:00:00 - return value at 06:00:00
member.param: The PIAcePoints of interest.
member.returns: PI event of the value and time stamp
member.remarks: updated 2009-03-31 10:30 NOTE from Elmer Botha 2009-07-13: This function must no longer be used as it only works for a totaliser tag. Rather use GetACE2TotValue as it accommodates all types of tags.

member.name: M:StreamMapperTotals.CommonFunctions.old_GetACE2TotValue(OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint,PITimeServer.PITime,System.Int64,System.Double)
member.summary: Gets the last value of a 2 point totaliser output, before or at the current execution time If time is 06:32 and the totaliser ends at 06:00:00 - return value at 06:00:00, OR if time is 06:00:00 and the totaliser ends at 06:00:00 - return value at 06:00:00
member.param: The PIAcePoint of interest.
The Execution time as PITime.
The period (in seconds as long) of the 2 point totlaiser been looked at - used for validation
2 point value match tolerance percent Abs((value1-value2)/Ave) less than or equal to Tol/100
member.returns: PI ace event with value and time, or nothing if the data is bad or does not exist
member.remarks: Warren: Was taken out of service on 2011-12-01 Note 2011-08-02 - Warren: should change this to use GetValueAtTime and feed in the start or end time of the Total updated 2009-10-27 - reduced the time span to look back, the the last totalisation period was bad the previous period was returned when it should not have been Note from Elmer Botha 2009-07-13: Use this function rather than GetTotValue. GetTotalValue only accomodates totaliser tags

member.name: M:StreamMapperTotals.CommonFunctions.old_20190515_GetACE2TotValue(OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint,PITimeServer.PITime,System.Int64,System.Double)
member.summary:
Update 2019-05-15: retired calc as some totalisers and ACE/AF calculations now only output one value at the start of the period and not 2 values.
Gets the last finalised value of a 2 point totaliser period, before or at the current execution time If a last finalised value does not exist in the last period, then nothing is returned If time is 06:32 and the totaliser ends at 06:00:00 - return value at 06:00:00, OR if time is 06:00:00 and the totaliser ends at 06:00:00 - return value at 06:00:00
member.param: The PIAcePoint of interest.
The Execution time as PITime.
The period (in seconds as long) of the 2 point totlaiser been looked at - used for validation
2 point value match tolerance percent Abs((value1-value2)/Ave) less than or equal to Tol/100
member.returns: PI ace event with value and time, or nothing if the data is bad or does not exist (no finalised value in the last period)

member.name: M:StreamMapperTotals.CommonFunctions.GetLastGood_Num(OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint,PITimeServer.PITime)
member.summary: Recurses back to get the last good (Neumeric) data event in the database
member.param: The PIAcePoint of interest - needs to be an floating point or integer.
The Execution time as PITime.
member.returns: Last good event as PIACEEvent in the database = nothing if non exists

member.name: M:StreamMapperTotals.CommonFunctions.GetLastGood(OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint,PITimeServer.PITime)
member.summary: Recurses back to get the last good data event in the historian
member.param: The PIAcePoint of interest.
The Execution time as PITime.
member.returns: Last good event as PIACEEvent in the database = nothing if non exists
member.remarks: updated 2010-06-17 15:24 re-writen 2013-05-03 Isgood(time) function was failing, chnaged to use pisdk to get compressed datab with a filter

member.name: M:StreamMapperTotals.CommonFunctions.GetNextGood_Num(OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint,PITimeServer.PITime)
member.remarks:
Recurses forward to get the first good (Neumeric) data event in the database
The PIAcePoint of interest.
The Execution time as PITime.
Next good PIACEEvent in the database = nothing if non exists
updated 2009-03-31 10:30 re-writen 2013-05-03 more effiecent method

member.name: M:StreamMapperTotals.CommonFunctions.GetNextGood(OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint,PITimeServer.PITime)
member.remarks:
Recurses forward to get the first good data event in the database
The PIAcePoint of interest.
The Execution time as PITime.
Next good PIACEEvent in the database = nothing if non exists
updated 2010-06-17 15:24 re-writen 2013-05-03 Isgood(time) function was failing, chnaged to use pisdk to get compressed datab with a filter

member.name: M:StreamMapperTotals.CommonFunctions.GenerateTotPeriodDates(PITimeServer.PITime,System.Int64,System.Int64)
member.summary: Generates two dates, the first of which is 1 second into the start of the totalisation period and the second is at the end of the totalisation period eg. GenerateTotPeriodDates(24 feb 05:00:01, 86400, 18000) yields 23 feb 05:00:01 and 24 feb 05:00:00 eg. GenerateTotPeriodDates(24 feb 05:00:01, 3600, 0) yields 24 feb 04:00:01 and 24 feb 05:00:00 The dates returned are for the first totalisation period that has closed (finalised) before the given date time
member.param: current/exicution time as pi time
The totalisation period in seconds as long = seconds between each totalisation period, 24 hrs = 86400
The offset of the totalisation period in seconds as long, 5 am = 18000
member.returns: DateTime array of: index 0 = time at 1 second into the start of the period and index 1 = time at the end of the period.

member.name: M:StreamMapperTotals.CommonFunctions.IterpolatedVal(OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint,PITimeServer.PITime)
member.summary: Value interpolated at the time given, numeric values only
member.param: The PIAcePoint of interest.
The Execution time as PItime.
member.returns: PIACE event of the interpolated value; = nothing if non exists
member.remarks: updated 2009-11-02 Is numeric check on result
